title: senecio jacobaea subsp. erucifolius
intro: Senecio jacobaea subsp. erucifolius, also known as ragwort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving coughs, and its extracts have been used in herbal preparations to create teas, infusions, and tinctures. However, due to its toxicity and potential risks, Senecio jacobaea subsp. erucifolius is often used under the guidance of a medical professional and in carefully controlled dosages.
